questions.question: A prospect replies to your cold email saying 'Not interested right now.' What stage should this deal be in?
questions.explanation: Any reply = engagement, even if negative. Move to Engaged, then follow up in 90 days with a new trigger. Don't close as Lost unless they explicitly say 'never contact me again.'

questions.question: How long should a deal typically stay in the 'Contacted' stage before you take action?

options.text: 3-7 days with follow-ups, then close as Lost if no response by Day 14
30 days minimum to give them time to think
Keep following up weekly for 90 days
Move to Lost immediately if they don't reply in 24 hours

questions.explanation: The 3-touch rule: follow up at Day 3 and Day 7. If no response by Day 14, move to Lost with reason 'No Response.' You can re-engage later with a new trigger.

questions.question: What's the most important field to require when closing a deal as Lost?
questions.explanation: Loss reason is strategic intelligence. If 60% of your losses are 'went with competitor X,' you have a positioning problem. Track this religiously.

questions.question: Solo founders should use separate pipelines for inbound vs. outbound leads.
questions.explanation: False. Use one pipeline with a 'Lead Source' field. Multiple pipelines fragment your reporting and double your maintenance work.

questions.question: A prospect had a great demo call and asked you to send pricing. What stage should this deal move to?

options.text: Keep in Meeting stage until they confirm interest
Move to Engaged stage to nurture more
Move to Won stage (they're clearly interested)

questions.explanation: Pricing sent = Proposal stage. Don't wait. The exit criteria for Meeting stage is 'proposal sent' — move it immediately and create a follow-up task.
